---
applyTo: "app/channels/**,app/**/*channel*.py,app/**/*flow*.py,app/**/*conversation*.py"
---

# SKILL 18 — UX & CONVERSATION DESIGN
## Source: `docs/skills/SKILL_ux.md`

---

## CORE UX PRINCIPLES

### PRINCIPLE 1 — ONE MESSAGE, ONE PURPOSE

Every bot message does one thing: ask one question OR confirm one action OR show one piece of info.
Never combine ask + confirm + summary in one message.

```
WRONG: "Aapka paracetamol add ho gaya! Koi aur cheez? Agar confirm karna ho to
        CONFIRM likhein. Aur delivery address bhi bata dain."

CORRECT: "Paracetamol 500mg × 10 strips add ho gaya ✅
          Koi aur medicine chahiye?"
```

### PRINCIPLE 2 — NEVER MAKE THE CUSTOMER GUESS

The next action must always be explicit. Customer must never wonder "ab kya karna hai?"

```
CORRECT: "Koi aur cheez chahiye? Ya bill dekhna hai?"
CORRECT: "Confirm karna ho to *CONFIRM* likhein 👆"
CORRECT: "1 likhein JazzCash ke liye, 2 likhein EasyPaisa ke liye"
```

### PRINCIPLE 3 — CONFIRM BEFORE WRITING

TELETRAAN NEVER writes data to DB without explicit customer confirmation:
1. Bot shows what it understood
2. Customer confirms or corrects
3. Bot writes to DB only after confirmation

```
After entity extraction:
"*Brufen 400mg* — 10 strips?  Haan/Nahi?"

After customer says "haan": Now add to order.
```

### PRINCIPLE 4 — FORGIVE TYPOS ALWAYS

Fuzzy matching happens silently. If bot not sure, it shows options.
Never say "medicine nahi mili" when fuzzy candidates exist.

```
WRONG:   "Yeh medicine hamare catalog mein nahi hai."
CORRECT: "Kya aap yeh mein se koi chahte hain?
          1. Paracetamol 500mg
          2. Paracetamol 125mg syrup
          3. Koi aur"
```

---

## ORDER FLOW (CHANNEL A)

```
Greeting → Item Collection → Confirmation → Payment → Complete
```

### Item Collection Loop
```
Customer sends medicine name/quantity
         ↓
Extract name + quantity (AI NLU)
         ↓
Fuzzy match in catalog
    ├── High confidence (>0.85): add silently, confirm added
    ├── Medium confidence (0.6–0.85): show match, ask confirm
    └── Low confidence (<0.6): show top 3 alternatives
         ↓
"Koi aur cheez chahiye? Ya order confirm karein?"
```

### Order Summary Format
```
📦 Aapka Order:

1. Paracetamol 500mg (GSK) × 10 strips  —  PKR 650
2. Amoxil 250mg Caps × 2 boxes          —  PKR 840
3. Brufen 400mg × 5 strips              —  PKR 225

─────────────────────────
Subtotal:   PKR 1,715
Discount:   PKR 85
Delivery:   PKR 100
─────────────────────────
Total:      PKR 1,730

Confirm karein? (CONFIRM / EDIT)
```

---

## ERROR RECOVERY

### Unclear input (intent = "unclear")
```
"Maafi, samajh nahi aaya. Kya aap medicine ka naam bata sakte hain?"
```

### Third unclear in a row → escalate
```
"Main theek se samajh nahi pa raha. Hamari team se baat karein —
abhi notify kar deta hoon. 🙏"
```

### Out of stock
```
"[Medicine] abhi stock mein nahi hai ⚠️
Kya aap order mein rakhna chahenge (available hone par dispatch hoga)?"
```

---

## BUTTON USAGE RULES

Use buttons when:
- Binary confirmation (Confirm / Edit, Yes / No)
- Payment method selection (max 3 options)
- Post-order actions (View Order, Contact Support)

Do NOT use buttons for:
- Medicine selection (too many items → use list or text)
- Any selection with more than 3 options

Button titles: max 20 chars, action-oriented ("✅ Confirm", "✏️ Edit", "❌ Cancel")
Button IDs: stable, descriptive, not sequential ("confirm_order", not "btn_1")
